zh.lifeSummary: 當Günther Vll von Schwarzburg in Blankenburg, Graf於大約1210年出生在德國時，他的父親Heinrich von Schwarzburg-Käfernburg, II49歲，母親Gräfin Irmgard von Orlamünde29歲。他和Sophie von Weimar-Orlamünde至少有5個兒子和7個女兒。他去世於1275年9月19日在他的家鄉享年66歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Günther Vll von Schwarzburg in Blankenburg, Graf于大约1210年出生在德国时，他的父亲Heinrich von Schwarzburg-Käfernburg, II49岁，母亲Gräfin Irmgard von Orlamünde29岁。他和Sophie von Weimar-Orlamünde至少有5个儿子和7个女儿。他去世于1275年9月19日在他的家乡享年66岁。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: in
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Kyung, Sung, Chan, Duk, Eun Suk, Hee, Hyun, Hyung, Ik, Jae, Kon, Kwang Won, Chang, Chung, Chong, Hyun Jin, Inki, Jeong, Jin Tae, Joo, Myong, Ok Soon, Poon, Poong.  Southeast Asian Hung, Khen, My, Phan, Tek, Thang, Tok.

Korean: name of a family that migrated from China to Korea during the reign of Shilla's 3rd-century king Sok Yu-rye (284–298). The founding ancestor, In So, established a clan seat in the Kyodong area of Cholla North Province. Another clan seat was subsequently established by his descendent, In Pin, during the Koryo period. The surname is not common in Korea.
Cambodian: written អ៊ិន, unexplained.

variants.name: Yin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Feng, Hong, Jian, Kong, Li, Man, Min, Ning, Yuan, Bei, Chao, Ching, Chong, Chung, Heng, Yeng, Tun, Youn.  Cambodian Hai, Phal, Phon, Pu, Samnang, Tho, Thy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 殷, meaning ‘plentiful’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Yin (殷), the name of the capital (located in present-day Henan province) of the Shang dynasty (1600–1046 BC ). Pan Geng, the 19th king of the Shang dynasty, moved the original capital Yan (located in present-day Shandong province) to Yin. Thus the Shang dynasty was also known as the Yin dynasty. After the Shang dynasty was overthrown by King Wu of Zhou (c. 1087–1043 BC ), people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from Yin Shui (殷水, ‘River Yin’), the name of a river in Shangshui in Henan Province. People who lived along the river acquired 殷 (Yin), the name of the river, as their surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 尹, a term for the official title in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Yin (尹), the name of a fief (said to have been located in Shexian in Shanxi province or Xin’an in Henan province), which was granted to Yin, the son of the legendary king Shao Hao (c. 26th century BC ). (ii) from the personal name of Shi Yin (師尹), literally meaning ‘head of officers’, the name of an official post during the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771 BC ). (iii) from the second element of Ling Yin (令尹), the name of an official post in charge of the military in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). This post was held by a prince of the state of Shen called Cheng, who migrated to the state of Chu when he took up the post.

nameMeanings.name: Blankenburg,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Heinz, Rainer.
German: habitational name from any of several places so called, in Brandenburg, the Harz Mountains, and Thuringia. Compare   Blankenberg  .

variants.name: Blankenberg
meanings: German and Dutch: habitational name from any of numerous places so named in Germany and the Netherlands. Compare   Blankenburg  .

nameMeanings.name: Graf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Horst, Manfred, Udo, Alois.

German (also Gräf): status name from Middle High German grāve, grābe, which was used as a title denoting various more or less aristocratic dignitaries and officials. In later times it became established as a title of nobility equivalent to the Romance count. The vocabulary word also denoted a variety of different minor local functionaries in different parts of Germany. In the Grand Duchy of Hesse, for example, it was used for the holder of the comparatively humble office of village headman (compare Mayer , Schulz , and Vogt ). The surname could have originated from any of these senses or be a metonymic occupational or status name for a servant or retainer of a count, or a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces. This surname is also found in many other European countries, for example in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of the corresponding Hungarian and Slavic surnames Gróf and Grof . Compare Graef .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name selected, like   Herzog   and other words denoting titles, because of their aristocratic connotations.

variants.name: Grebe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Fritz, Hans, Willi.
German (Hesse): variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: De Graaf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Adriaan, Bastiaan, Cornelis, Dirk, Geert.

Dutch: from graaf ‘count’ (see Graf ), with the addition of the definite article de ‘the’, hence a nickname for the servant of a count or for someone supposedly resembling one, and also a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn named with this word.

variants.name: Graefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Arno, Goetz, Horst, Johannes.
German (North Rhine-Westphalia; also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Grefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erhardt, Gerhard, Gerhart, Kurt.
North German: variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: Greve
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Otto, Reinhold, Bernhardt, Claus, Erna, Erwin, Ewald, Fritz, Kurt.
North German and Scandinavian: status name from Middle Low German and Danish greve ‘count’, equivalent to German Graf .
Dutch: vowel variant of Grave ‘count’, or a topographic name from Middle Dutch greve ‘ditch, moat, channel’.

variants.name: Croff
meanings: Altered form of German   Graf   or   Graff  . Or of Dutch   Kroft  .

variants.name: Mayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Franz, Helmut, Wilhelm, Erwin, Gerhard, Heinz, Ilse, Lorenz, Wolfgang.

English (Staffordshire): post-medieval form of Mear, which is either a topographic name, from Middle English mere ‘pond, pool’ (Old English mere) or Middle English mere ‘boundary’ (Old English (ge)mǣre), or a habitational name from one or more of the many places with names derived from these terms, such as Mere (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Wiltshire), Meir in Caverswall (Staffordshire), and Meare (Somerset).
English: occupational name for a physician, from Old French mire, Middle English mire, meir, meyre, originally synonymous with Myer .

variants.name: Grafe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Ralf.
German (also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Graef
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Egon, Lothar, Otto, Armin, Claus, Erwin, Franz, Friedrich, Hans, Helmut, Hermann, Jochen.
German (also Gräf): see Graf .
Dutch: variant of Graaf, see De Graaf .

variants.name: Graff
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Gräff): variant of Graf . Compare Graeff .

English (London): metonymic occupational name for a clerk or scribe, from Old French grafe ‘stylus, pencil’, a derivative of Old French grafer ‘to write’ (from Late Latin grafare, from Greek graphein). It is uncertain whether the English name survived the medieval period or whether the early 17th century bearers in Britain were in fact of German origin.

nameMeanings.name: Günther
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from gund ‘strife’ + heri ‘army’; name of a tragic hero in the Nibelungenlied and in Wagner's Ring cycle.

variants.name: Günter
meanings: from gund ‘strife’ + heri ‘army’; name of a tragic hero in the Nibelungenlied and in Wagner's Ring cycle.

variants.name: Gunter
meanings: from gund ‘strife’ + heri ‘army’; name of a tragic hero in the Nibelungenlied and in Wagner's Ring cycle.

nameMeanings.name: Vll
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Valerie  , and sometimes also of   Valentine .

variants.name: Valerie
meanings: From the French form of the Latin name Valeria, feminine of Valerius, an old Roman family name apparently derived from valere ‘to be healthy, strong’. The name owes its popularity as a male name in France to the cult of a 3rd-century saint who was converted to Christianity by Martial of Limoges. The masculine form Valery is found occasionally in England in the 16th century, but by the 17th century had fallen into disuse.

variants.name: Valentine
meanings: form of the Latin name Valentinus, a derivative of valens ‘healthy, strong’. This was the name of a Roman martyr of the 3rd century, whose feast is celebrated on 14 February . This was the date of a pagan fertility festival marking the first stirrings of spring, which has survived in an attenuated form under the patronage of the saint.

en.lifeSummary: When Günther Vll von Schwarzburg in Blankenburg, Graf was born about 1210, in Germany, his father, Heinrich von Schwarzburg-Käfernburg, II, was 49 and his mother, Gräfin Irmgard von Orlamünde, was 29. He had at least 5 sons and 7 daughters with Sophie von Weimar-Orlamünde. He died on 19 September 1275, in his hometown, at the age of 66.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Günther Vll von Schwarzburg in Blankenburg, Graf est né environ 1210, à Allemagne, son père, Heinrich von Schwarzburg-Käfernburg, II, avait 49 ans et sa mère, Gräfin Irmgard von Orlamünde, avait 29 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils et 7 filles avec Sophie von Weimar-Orlamünde. Il est décédé le 19 septembre 1275, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 66 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Günther Vll von Schwarzburg in Blankenburg, Graf um 1210 in Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Heinrich von Schwarzburg-Käfernburg, II 49 und seine Mutter Gräfin Irmgard von Orlamünde 29. Er hatte mit Sophie von Weimar-Orlamünde mindestens 5 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Er starb am 19. September 1275 im Alter von 66 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Günther Vll von Schwarzburg in Blankenburg, Graf è nato il circa 1210 nel Germania, il suo padre, Heinrich von Schwarzburg-Käfernburg, II, aveva 49 anni e il suo madre, Gräfin Irmgard von Orlamünde, aveva 29 anni. Lui avuto almeno 5 figli e 7 figlie con Sophie von Weimar-Orlamünde. Morì il 19 settembre 1275, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 66 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Günther Vll von Schwarzburg in Blankenburg, Grafが約1210年にドイツで生まれた時，父Heinrich von Schwarzburg-Käfernburg, IIは49歳，母Gräfin Irmgard von Orlamündeは29歳であった。彼はSophie von Weimar-Orlamündeとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と7人の娘をもうけた。彼は1275年9月19日に故郷で66歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Günther Vll von Schwarzburg in Blankenburg, Graf(이)가 대략 1210년 독일에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Heinrich von Schwarzburg-Käfernburg, II(은)는 49세였고, 그의 어머니 Gräfin Irmgard von Orlamünde(은)는 29세였다. 그는 Sophie von Weimar-Orlamünde(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1275년 9월 19일 그의 고향에서 66세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Günther Vll von Schwarzburg in Blankenburg, Graf nasceu aproximadamente 1210, em Alemanha, seu pai, Heinrich von Schwarzburg-Käfernburg, II, tinha 49 anos e sua mãe, Gräfin Irmgard von Orlamünde, tinha 29 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 7 filhas com Sophie von Weimar-Orlamünde. Ele faleceu em 19 de setembro de 1275, em sua cidade natal, com 66 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Günther Vll von Schwarzburg in Blankenburg, Graf родился около 1210 г. в местности Германия, его отцу по имени Heinrich von Schwarzburg-Käfernburg, II было 49 лет, а матери, которую звали Gräfin Irmgard von Orlamünde, 29. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 7 дочерей от Sophie von Weimar-Orlamünde. Он умер 19 сентября 1275 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 66 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Günther Vll von Schwarzburg in Blankenburg, Graf nació aproximadamente 1210, en Alemania, su papá, Heinrich von Schwarzburg-Käfernburg, II, tenía 49 años y su mamá, Gräfin Irmgard von Orlamünde, tenía 29 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 7 hijas con Sophie von Weimar-Orlamünde. Murió el 19 de septiembre de 1275, en su pueblo, a la edad de 66 años.
